title: Open Shelf
summary: Add the perfect decorative accent to your home with this unique open shelf, great for displaying books, plants, or your favorite home decor.
description: Add the perfect decorative accent to your home with this unique open shelf, great for displaying books, plants, or your favorite home decor. This fast and easy beginner DIY build will look great in any space!

steps.title: Cut Wood
steps.body: Using a miter saw cut your wood to the specified sizes.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Drill 2 1/2'' pocket holes. Each shelf board will need 4 pocket holes on ONE side. The divider board will need 4 pocket holes alternating two on each side.

steps.title: Attach divider board to base
steps.body: Using 2 1/2'' pocket hole screws and wood glue attach the divider board to the center of the base.

steps.title: Attach shelves to divider board
steps.body: Using 2 1/2'' pocket hole screws and wood glue attach the shelves to the divider board as shown. For easy placement use scrap spacer boards, one cut at 12'' and one at 17''. Use a right angle clamp or a bar clamp to hole the shelf in place while drilling in the screws.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body:
Fill desired pocket holes with wood filler or pocket hole plugs, then sand smooth and clean off the dust. Apply the paint or stain of your choice. Then enjoy!
*Recommend: No more than 20 lbs per shelf. If you are worried about stability you can add an L bracket underneath a shelf at the back and attach it to the wall for safety. I have not had any issues with my shelf feeling unstable, but it would be an easy fix if you are concerned.
